ON ASBESTOS TRIAL MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, APRIL 26, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., ARGUED, AND PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR PREFERENCE IS GRANTED PER CCP 36(A) AND (D). PLAINTIFFS AGREED THAT SUMMARY MOTIONS MAY BE HEARD ON 15 DAYS NOTICE WITH AN ADDITIONAL TWO DAYS FOR ESERVICE. THE TRIAL IS SET FOR AUGUST 15, 2011 AT 11:15AM IN DEPARTMENT 220. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY MOTIONS IS AUGUST 3. PLAINTIFFS TO MAKE A DEMAND ON ALL DEFENDANTS NO LATER THAN JULY 27. MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE ON AUGUST 3 AT 9AM IN ROOM 603. A CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET ON 5/10/11 AT 2:30 P.M. IN DEPT. 220. TELEPHONIC APPEARANCES ON 5/10/11 ARE APPROVED. PARTIES ARE ORDER TO MEET AND CONFER BEFORE FILING ANY DISCOVERY MOTIONS. PARTIES AGREED TO THE FOLLOWING: RESPONSE TO DISCOVERY IS SHORTENED TO 20 DAYS, DISCOVERY IS CLOSED AT TRIAL ASSIGNMENT, ECONOMIC DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS IS DUE 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE, PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL IDENTIFICATION AND LAY WITNESSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS THAT SUPPORT THEIR TESTIMONY, AS TO EACH INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT BY 30 DAYS AFTER THE GRANTING OF PREFERENCE, ALL DEFENDANTS SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL LAY WITNESSES THAT THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT LEARNED OF THROUGH ITS OWN DISCOVERY EFFORTS AND THAT WILL RELY ON PROPOSITION 51 PURPOSES OR FOR THEIR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES BY 50 DAYS AFTER THE GRANTING OF PREFERENCE. MOVING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: D. LOK; MATTER NOT REPORTED (220)

TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT ROCKWELL AUTOMATION, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF ALMA JEANNETTE BENNETT TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY IS GRANTED AS TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION NOS. 23-101 AND TAKEN OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 27 AND REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION NOS. 1, 16-19 AND 21. AS TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION NOS. 23-101, PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES DO NOT COMPLY WITH C.C.P. 2033.220(B). PLAINTIFF HAS 10 DAYS TO SERVE SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES. DEFENDANT'S MOVING PAPERS DID NOT PROVIDE NOTICE THAT IT WAS REQUESTING SANCTIONS SO ITS REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS THAT WAS ONLY INCLUDED IN ITS REPLY IS DENIED. (SEE C.C.P. 2023.030, 2023.040.) DEFENDANT REPRESENTED THAT THE MOTION IS NOW MOOT AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 27 AND REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION NOS. 1, 16-19 AND 21. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, MATTER NOT REPORTED

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT GRUNDFOS CBS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. NO APPEARANCE AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: GRANTED. DEFENDANT SUSTAINED ITS INITIAL BURDEN UNDER AGUILAR AND PLAINTIFFS' LATE EVIDENCE WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED BY THE COURT, THUS PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER MR. BENNETT WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT'S UNTIMELINESS OBJECTIONS TO THE OPPOSITION ARE SUSTAINED. PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 437C, PLAINTIFFS' OPPOSITION WAS DUE BY AUGUST 14. PLAINTIFFS' PROOF OF SERVICE DEMONSTRATES THAT ITS OPPOSITION WAS NOT SERVED UNTIL AUGUST 17. PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE GOOD CAUSE FOR THE LATE FILING AND SERVICE OF THEIR OPPOSITION AND ALL ATTACHED PAPERS. (SEE HOBSON V. RAYCHEM CORP. (1999) 73 CAL.APP.4TH 614, 623-625 (DISAPPROVED ON OTHER GROUNDS IN COLMENARES V. BRAEMAR COUNTRY CLUB, INC. (2003) 29 CAL.4TH 1019).) ALTHOUGH NOT MAKING A RULING, THE COURT NOTES THAT EVEN IF IT HAD BEEN ABLE TO CONSIDER PLAINTIFFS' EVIDENCE, SUMMARY JUDGMENT WOULD BE GRANTED BECAUSE PLAINTIFFS' EVIDENCE FAILED TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER MR. BENNETT WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, MATTER NOT REPORTED

RULING:
REHEARING ON DEFENDANT HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Zac Baker of K&L GATES LLP (415-882-8200), appearing for defendant / moving party Schneider Electric USA, Inc.

RULING:
DEFENDANT SCHNEIDER ELECTRIC USA, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Schneider Electric USA, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Bennett was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to Mr. Powell's deposition testimony are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-18-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jeffrey T. Thayer of DEHAY ELLISTON LLP (510-285-0750), appearing for defendants / moving parties Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.
Stephen Blackburn of DEBLASE BROWN EYERLY LLP (310-575-9955), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Raymond Gene Bennett, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANTS HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC. AND KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendants Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendants sustained their initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Bennett was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendants. In their opposition plaintiffs conceded that there is insufficient evidence to prevail at trial against Hanson Permanente. Defendants' objections to Mr. Powell's deposition testimony and to Kaiser Gypsum's interrogatory responses are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Bobbie Jo Harr of HG LITIGATION SERVICES, CSR #6090 (503/TLJ)

Off Calendar Aug-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Joseph Gunter of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party Oakfabco, Inc.
Stephen Blackburn of DEBLASE BROWN EYERLY LLP (310-575-9955), appearing for plaintiffs /
/ opposing parties Raymond Gene Bennett, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT OAKFABCO, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Oakfabco, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Bennett was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to deft. The court only considered the evidence submitted with plaintiffs' initial opposition. The court could not consider the evidence submitted with plaintiffs' supplemental opposition because it was untimely pursuant to C.C.P. section 437c and pltfs failed to demonstrate good cause for the late filing and service of their supplemental opposition and all attached papers. (See Hobson v. Raychem Corp. (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 614, 623-625 (disapproved on other grounds in Colmenares v. Braemar Country Club, Inc. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1019).) Defendant's objections to the untimely supplemental opposition are sustained. Defendant's objections to A.P. Green's interrogatory responses are sustained. The court did not consider any of the deposition testimony of Mr. Bennett. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

Defendant Grundfos Cbs, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-28-2012 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-21-2012 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-28-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Grundfos CBS, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden under Aguilar and plaintiffs' late evidence will not be considered by the court, thus plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Bennett was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's untimeliness objections to the opposition are sustained. Pursuant to C.C.P. section 437c, plaintiffs' opposition was due by August 14. Plaintiffs' proof of service demonstrates that its opposition was not served until August 17. Plaintiffs failed to demonstrate good cause for the late filing and service of their opposition and all attached papers. (See Hobson v. Raychem Corp. (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 614, 623-625 (disapproved on other grounds in Colmenares v. Braemar Country Club, Inc. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1019).) Although not making a ruling, the court notes that even if it had been able to consider plaintiffs' evidence, summary judgment would be granted because plaintiffs' evidence failed to create a triable issue whether Mr. Bennett was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Prevailing party to prepare an order. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
